UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DANNY SCHER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS RE: (1) FORESEEABILITY OF HARM; (2) APPLICABILITY OF CCC 846; AND (3) PREMISES BEING PUBLIC PROPERTY; (4) EXISTENCE OF LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT FOR UNCERTAINTY. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION. NUISANCE IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED AT THIS STAGE OF THE PLEADINGS. DEFENDANT PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DANNY SCHER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS RE: MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD. DEFENDANT SHALL MAKE PROPER CITATIONS TO AUTHORITIES IN FUTURE. SEE MOVING PAPERS 6:13-14; 9:18). DEFENDANT PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice And Motion To Vacate Void Judgment Of March 21, 2014 On Grounds Of Defendant Misconduct
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff CONSTANCE BOURGES, Tel No. 415-272-1577 appearing in pro per;
Atty. DAVID W. CHEN, ESQ. of Stratman, PAtterson & Hunter, Tel No. 510-457-3440 appearing for the Defendant Danny Scher.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BOURGES' Motion To Vacate Void Judgment Of March 21, 2014 On Grounds Of Defendant Misconduct is denied. There is no good cause to vacate the judgment and the court has already denied the instant motion on multiple occasions. Plaintiff should seek appellate relief and no longer file this repetitive motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Pltf'S Vacate Judgment Deft'S 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Complaint/Show Burden Of Proof For Ccp 473 (B) Excusable Negligence /Timeliness Issue- Accept 5th Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-25-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Constance Bourges, in pro per, Tel. 415.272.1577, for plaintiffs;
Brennain Garber, of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 530.400.2670, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BOURGES' Motion For Plaintiff's Vacate Judgment Defendant's 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Complaint/Show Burden Of Proof For CCP 473 (B) Excusable Negligence /Timeliness Issue- Accept 5th Amended Complaint is denied. There is no good cause to vacate the judgment. The court denies defendant's sanctions request. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/SRB)

Notice And Motion For Plaintiff'S Vacate Judgement Defendant'S 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgement On Sanctions Based On Ccp 473.B And Cr 3.110(F) Declaration Of Additional Evidence Fifth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff CONSTANCE BOURGES, Tel No. (415) 272-1577 present in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Plaintiff did not timely notify the Court or opposing side of her intent to appear today. The Court permitted the Plaintiff to present her arguments to the Court. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BOURGES' Motion to Vacate Judgment on Defendant's 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgment on Sanctions Based On CCP 473.B And Cr 3.110(F) Declaration Of Additional Evidence Fifth Amended Complaint, the Motions to Vacate are DENIED. The motions are untimely and in any event do not show mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect. Defendant's request for sanctions regarding these motions is DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment Defendant'S 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgment On Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-08-2014 Continued To Oct-15-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Constance Bourges, Plaintiff in pro per and moving party, (415-272-1577).
David W. Chen, Esq. of Stratman Patterson & Hunter, (510-457-3442), for Danny Scher, Defendant and opposing party.

Hearing is held. Argued briefly on the merits of the proof of service on demurrer to the fourth amended complaint. Court rules that until the proof is obtained proving that the demurrer to the fourth amended complaint was filed with the Court, no ruling will be tendered in this motion. Court adopts part of the tentative ruling denying plaintiff's peremptory challenge and rules that PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BOURGES' Motion To Vacate Judgment Defendant's 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff's 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgment On Sanctions, is now continued to October 15, 2014 at 9:30am in Dept. 501. Mr. Chen, defendant's counsel is ordered to give Notice of new hearing date to plaintiff in pro per. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

Off Calendar Oct-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Plaintiff Constance Bourges present (415-272-1577) No other appearance. (Plaintiff did not notify defendant about her appearance)

Tenative ruling is adopted, DEFENDANT DANNY SCHER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part.
